get_work->retry->queue_payload+mark_get_block_pending bodies are mirror copies; differ only in GetBlockReason enum and 'Accepted-block'/'Rejected-block' log string. Same commit factored other GET_BLOCK sites into request_and_queue_get_block helper but left these two handlers duplicated

false_positives.what_happened: every TEST setup repeats the identical fixture-construction scaffold (make_node_session(io_context, config, socket, "TEST_X") preceded by io_context/config/socket boilerplate). Line/token detector sees these near-duplicate sequential setup blocks as within-file copies, but it is standard per-test harness setup, not logic drift
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: down-weight/ignore duplicate blocks that are dominated by a single fixture/factory construction call and contain no branching or arithmetic. Concretely: require a flagged block to contain >=1 control-flow token (if/for/while/switch) or a minimum count of distinct call targets; suppress blocks that are just sequential variable-init + one shared helper call inside test functions

false_positives.what_happened: after this commit factored the helper, the many request_and_queue_get_block(connection, <Reason>, "[Solo ...] GET_BLOCK") call sites are near-identical 3-arg invocations differing only by enum + log string. A token detector flags them as repeated chunks, but they are intentional single-line helper calls (the de-dup target), not copy-paste
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: set a minimum chunk size in statements/lines (e.g. >=4 non-trivial statements) AND exclude runs that are a single call expression spanning multiple lines due to argument wrapping; normalize multi-line call-arg formatting to a single logical statement before length thresholding
